content: FORM 1099-DA — NEW IRS REPORTING RULES (2025-2026): Starting 2025: Digital asset brokers must report GROSS PROCEEDS from crypto dispositions to IRS. Starting 2026: Reporting expands to include COST BASIS, acquisition dates, holding periods, and short-term vs long-term capital gains classification. Box 1i specifically reports WASH SALE losses — signaling imminent wash sale rule enforcement for crypto. Impact: Every trade on Kraken will be reported to the IRS. You CANNOT hide gains. Maintain meticulous records of every trade, including fees, to accurately calculate cost basis.

WASH SALE RULE COMING TO CRYPTO: The wash sale rule prevents deducting a capital loss if a substantially identical asset is repurchased within 30 days. Historically only applied to securities, NOT crypto. The 1099-DA form includes a wash sale reporting field = definitive legislative intent to enforce on crypto. Impact: Tax-loss harvesting strategies (sell BTC at loss, immediately rebuy) will become ILLEGAL. Workaround: Wait 31+ days before repurchasing. Or swap to a NON-identical asset (sell BTC, buy ETH, wait 31 days, swap back). Implementation timeline: Likely enforced starting 2026 or 2027 tax year.

COST BASIS TRACKING — FIFO vs LIFO vs Specific Identification: FIFO (First In, First Out): Oldest coins sold first. Default method. In bull markets, FIFO produces HIGHER gains (selling oldest, cheapest coins). LIFO (Last In, First Out): Newest coins sold first. In bull markets, LIFO produces LOWER gains (selling newest, most expensive coins). Specific Identification: Choose WHICH coins to sell. Most tax-efficient but requires meticulous record-keeping. Rule: Use a crypto tax tool (Koinly, CoinTracker, TokenTax) to track cost basis across ALL wallets and exchanges. NEVER rely on exchange reports alone — they miss cross-exchange transfers.

ENTITY STRUCTURING FOR TRADING OPERATIONS: LLC: Liability protection, pass-through taxation, flexible profit allocation. S-Corp: Can pay yourself a 'reasonable salary' and take remaining profits as distributions (avoids self-employment tax on distributions). For active traders: Consider Trader Tax Status (TTS) with IRS — allows deducting trading expenses, mark-to-market election, and home office deduction. Requirements: Must trade substantially continuously, seek profit from daily market movements, and spend significant time trading. State considerations: Kentucky flat 4.0% income tax (dropping to 3.5% in 2026) is favorable for trading entities.

TAX-LOSS HARVESTING BEFORE WASH SALE ENFORCEMENT: While wash sale rules don't yet apply to crypto (but will soon), harvest losses aggressively: 1) Sell positions at a loss to offset realized gains. 2) Immediately repurchase (currently legal for crypto). 3) Net effect: reduce taxable gains while maintaining position. IMPORTANT: This window is CLOSING. Once wash sale rules apply, you'll need to wait 31 days. Strategy: Harvest losses during bear markets (ExtremeFear) when positions are most likely underwater. Keep records of every harvest for when rules change. Don't let the tax tail wag the trading dog — only harvest if the trade makes sense independently.
